response.step_by_step_log: 1. Analyzed the video and identified two distinct musical performances. 2. Identified 'Grateful Days' by Dragon Ash as the first song and 'Lupin the Fire' by SEAMO as the second. 3. Used web search to confirm song titles, composers, and performers (Inugami Korone and others). 4. Searched for and obtained the full original Japanese lyrics and English translations for both songs. 5. Mapped the scenes based on music and transitions (00:00-03:22, 03:23-03:50, 03:50-04:55). 6. Compiled all metadata and lyrics into the required JSON format.
response.global_summary: A performance video featuring VTubers (Inugami Korone and others) singing 'Grateful Days' by Dragon Ash and 'Lupin the Fire' by SEAMO.

scenes.description: The performance of 'Grateful Days' by Dragon Ash, featuring multiple VTubers, including Inugami Korone.

scenes.reference_lyrics_en:
Turn up the radio, I can hear it again today too, the stereo sways in the wind.
The rhythm is carved into my shoulder, and my feelings ride on the rhyme.
Right under the clear sky, we headed toward the far away future.
Grasping the master key tightly and departing, one kick down in the hustle and bustle.
Now I think about those days of fighting, and I feel compassion overflowing with thanks.
That battle that I fought so desperately in still continues now, and the front line is right here.
I got my unfaltering pride from my father, my mother gave me my large amount of consideration.
From you, I get warmth; it's my provision, so that I can live through 'til tomorrow.

I got sound. I got feel. I got beautiful days.
I got song. I got love. I got grateful days.
I got it.
You make me happy, when sky's gray.
Darling, my darling.
Thank you father, mother, and my friend.

I was born in Tokyo and raised on hip hop, my friends were generally guys that seemed bad.
With these same guys that seemed bad, I walked back roads, we came to see these cities.
Shibuya, Roppongi; that's right, puberty hit me early.
I kept leaving my backpacks at school.
I really got in trouble with my parents.
But time passed, and now I'm on magazine covers.
And I throw my weight around all over, don dada.
If I've got a mic, I'm really a no. 1 top ranker who represents Tokyo.
Yeah, I was given life in this world, this country; I'm unrivaled when I've got the mic.
Today I'm advancing on the rough off-road, giving thanks to my friends, family, and fans.

I got sound. I got feel. I got beautiful days.
I got song. I got love. I got grateful days.
I got it.
You make me happy, when sky's gray.
Darling, my darling.
Thank you father, mother, and my friend.

We were born and raised in the land of the rising sun, and now we stand firmly on the ground, like this.
The page is always open, let's set off for our separate stages.
We play music about the joy and gratitude that we make here, and truly understand it.
We portray the era without hiding anything; pushing aside the pressure, an irregular reflection.
I'm going to pour in some more overflowing sentiments, and one day some unextinguishable love.
I dive into the battlefield without hesitation, a crest of lilies blooms without withering.
A gentle wind blows by in the wearing afternoon, a rainbow peeks out after the rain.
The sunlight is reflected in a puddle; I praise the happiness of these days.
The pounding of my heart goes into song.
I feel the things that are here in these days, in my hands.

I got sound. I got feel. I got beautiful days.
I got song. I got love. I got grateful days.
I got it.
You make me happy, when sky's gray.
Darling, my darling.
Thank you father, mother, and my friend.
